items.headline: Mountain View updates biodiversity and urban forest strategy
items.summary: The City Council reviewed a draft Biodiversity and Urban Forest Plan on April 28, which will guide how Mountain View manages trees, green spaces, and wildlife habitat. This plan affects what trees get planted, how parks are developed, and environmental quality across the city.

items.headline: Mixed-use development with 67 homes and retail planned for Sunnyvale
items.summary: The Planning Commission reviewed a proposed 4.22-acre mixed-use project that would add 55 townhomes and 12 apartment units alongside 18,535 square feet of retail space, including a new two-story building while preserving the existing Speedy's Tacos location. The project requires subdivision into 17 individual lots and represents significant new residential and commercial development on the site.

items.headline: Protected tree removal permit at 751 Waverley under review
items.summary: A property owner's approved protected tree removal permit is undergoing director review, suggesting either community concern or complications with the initial approval process.

items.headline: Campbell increases business license tax rates
items.summary: The City Council approved an ordinance revising business license tax rates in the Campbell Municipal Code, effective for the new fiscal year. The rate change affects all businesses operating in Campbell and represents the city's annual update to licensing fees.

items.headline: FIFA World Cup 2026: Santa Clara approves temporary supergraphic signs near Levi's Stadium
items.summary: The City Council authorized large promotional signs on building surfaces around Levi's Stadium during the 2026 FIFA World Cup events. This is part of preparations for hosting matches at the stadium during the international tournament.

items.headline: Milpitas updates development fees and user charges for FY 2026-27
items.summary: The City Council approved new development impact fees and municipal user charges that will take effect in the next fiscal year. These fee changes affect costs for new construction projects and city services, potentially impacting housing development timelines and resident utility bills.

items.headline: Santa Clara County presents homelessness action plan to Milpitas Council
items.summary: County officials presented their Community Plan to End Homelessness to the City Council on April 21st. The presentation outlined regional strategies that may affect local services and resources dedicated to addressing homelessness in Milpitas.

items.headline: City Council presents guide to building permits and development timelines
items.summary: The council reviewed a comprehensive guide explaining San José's permitting process, development timelines, and coordination requirements for residents and developers. This resource could help clarify how long projects take and what to expect when building in the city.
